query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "translation as a bridging action: transferring manipulation skills fro" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it provides direct evidence on the mechanism of transferring manipulation skills from simulation to reality using world-action models, which is the core subject of the user's query regarding "Translation as a Bridging Action" and skill transfer. Although the user's title fragment mentions "Translation," the context of "manipulation skills" and the candidate paper's focus on "Sim-to-Real Transfer" confirms they address the same domain of robotic skill
